dataset: THINGS H I robust-weighted moment maps cross-matched to M9 SPARC
proxy_level: M9 activation transferred to THINGS H I structure proxies
headline_metric: Spearman(S_HI_disturbance, A_gov_inferred) = -0.283; low-A median disturbance 0.700 vs high-A 0.550
primary_result: Cross-matched 8 THINGS galaxies to M9 SPARC. Spearman(S_HI_disturbance, A_gov_inferred)=-0.012; median low-A disturbance=0.525; median high-A disturbance=0.625.
interpretation: Current M11 rerun challenges the simple low-activation/higher-H I-disturbance proxy expectation in the tiny THINGS overlap; treat as a pilot falsifier signal, not validation.
claim_boundary: This is an external diagnostic using computed H I structure proxies and M9 activation transfer; it is not a direct refit of BDVR to THINGS rotation curves.

dataset: MaNGA DR17 DAPall/DRPall scalar proxy catalog
headline_metric: N=10185; Spearman(A_proxy, surface density)=0.367; Spearman(A_proxy, sSFR)=-0.391
primary_result: Computed MaNGA governor proxy for 10185 galaxies. Spearman(A_proxy, stellar_surface_density)=0.367; Spearman(A_proxy, specific_sfr)=-0.391; median disk-like A_proxy=0.5028636884306987; median spheroid-like A_proxy=0.517321224022255.
interpretation: Large-N proxy coherence: compact, less star-forming systems rank higher in proxy activation.
claim_boundary: This is a MaNGA large-sample governor proxy diagnostic, not a direct BDVR activation, not a SPARC-like flat-velocity fit, and not a dark-matter-law validation.

headline_metric: N=260; fast-rotator median bridge 0.551 vs slow-rotator 0.186
primary_result: Computed ATLAS3D mixed-support bridge proxy for 260 early-type galaxies. Spearman(bridge, lambda_R)=0.951; Spearman(bridge, V/sigma)=0.939; Spearman(bridge, surface density)=0.408; median fast-rotator bridge=0.5507692307692309; median slow-rotator bridge=0.18615384615384617.
interpretation: Fast rotators occupy the expected mixed rotation/dispersion bridge branch.
claim_boundary: This is an ATLAS3D mixed rotation/dispersion bridge proxy, not a disk BDVR activation, not A_gov_inferred, and not a per-galaxy tuned model.

headline_metric: N=260; slow-rotator median pressure response 0.750 vs fast-rotator 0.469
primary_result: Computed ATLAS3D pressure-channel proxy for 260 early-type galaxies. Spearman(pressure response, pressure_support)=0.814; Spearman(pressure response, stellar_surface_density)=0.302; Spearman(pressure response, lambda_R)=-0.817; median slow-rotator pressure response=0.7499999999999999; median fast-rotator pressure response=0.4692307692307692.
interpretation: Pressure-supported systems require a separate observable channel from disk rotation.
claim_boundary: Ellipticals require a separate observable mapping. This is an ATLAS3D pressure-channel proxy, not v_flat, not A_gov_inferred, not a disk BDVR activation, and not direct lensing/X-ray/dynamical-excess validation.

primary_result: External maturity proxy vs f_gas Spearman=-0.950; vs M_H2/M_HI Spearman=0.373; vs H2 depletion time Spearman=0.290.
interpretation: Gas depletion, stellar dominance, and molecular organization follow the expected maturity ordering.
claim_boundary: This is an external diagnostic test, not a validated evolutionary reconstruction, and not a direct rotation-curve test.
